topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment was suddenly deafening, punctuated only by the frantic drumming of my own pulse. I found the evidence tucked inside a forgotten textbook—a meticulously annotated grammar book, filled with handwritten notes in a script I recognized instantly. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the ache in my molars. When he walked in, whistling tunelessly, I didn't look up. I just stood by the kitchen counter, gripping a mug until my knuckles were white, the steam rising in ragged, shallow puffs. "What is this?" My voice came out dangerously low, a tight wire about to snap. He stopped mid-stride, the whistling dying in his throat. The shift in his posture, the sudden stillness, told me everything I needed to know. I finally lifted my gaze, and the cold, sharp accusation in my eyes made him visibly shrink.

[story 2]

It was the sheer audacity of it that made my hands shake. I stared at the flashcards scattered across the coffee table, the unfamiliar characters mocking me. Every line, every inflection he’d been practicing, was a deliberate, quiet invasion of the space we shared. A hot, prickly sensation crawled up my neck, making my skin crawl. I slammed the book shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet living room. "Stop," I managed, the word brittle and strained. I paced three steps toward the window, my breath catching in short, sharp gasps. I watched him, trying to read the panic flickering in his eyes, the immediate realization that he’d been caught. I felt a burning pressure building behind my ribs, a need to shout, to shatter the fragile peace with a single, explosive sound.

[story 3]

I walked into the bedroom, the air thick and heavy. There, on his desk, were recordings—tapes of him practicing dialogue, the cadence undeniably native, flowing with an effortless grace that felt like a betrayal. My vision narrowed. My breath hitched, shallow and quick, as I circled the desk slowly, my movements jerky and uncontrolled. I didn't speak immediately. Instead, I picked up a heavy ceramic vase, the weight grounding me momentarily, but the tension coiled tighter within my core. When I finally spoke, my voice was dangerously level, stripped bare of warmth. "You thought I wouldn't notice?" The quiet intensity of my tone seemed to suck all the air out of the room. He finally looked up, and the look in his eyes was a mixture of shame and a stubborn defiance that only intensified the churning turmoil inside me.
